plans.noPlansAdmin: No plans yet. Click "Add Plan" to create one.
plans.noPlansUser: No plans available. Contact your administrator.
app.networkPolicy.title: Network Policy
app.networkPolicy.description: Configure network traffic rules for your application. Changes will take effect after the next deployment. The default setting for an app is "Allow All", which allows traffic to and from all apps within the same project and the internet.
app.networkPolicy.enableDescription: Control whether network policies are applied to this application
app.networkPolicy.disabledWarning: Disabling network policies removes all network traffic restrictions for this application. This may expose your application to unauthorized access and security risks. Only disable this if you fully understand the security implications.
app.networkPolicy.ingressHelp: Controls who can connect to your pods.
app.networkPolicy.egressHelp: Controls where your pods can connect to.
app.networkPolicy.typesDescription: Understand how each policy type controls traffic to and from your application.
app.networkPolicy.allowAllDescription: Allows traffic from or to all apps within the same project and the internet. External internet traffic reaches your app through the Traefik ingress controller. Blocks traffic from or to other projects and namespaces.
app.networkPolicy.internetOnlyDescriptio: Allows traffic only from or to the internet via the Traefik ingress controller. Blocks all direct pod-to-pod communication within the cluster, including same-project apps. Useful for public-facing applications that should not communicate with internal services.
app.networkPolicy.projectAppsOnlyDescrip: Allows traffic only from or to apps within the same project. Blocks all internet traffic and traffic from other projects. Ideal for internal microservices that should only communicate within your project.
app.networkPolicy.denyAllDescription: Blocks all incoming or outgoing traffic. Use this for maximum isolation when your application should not communicate with any other service.
app.source.deployHint: Click "deploy" to apply the changes to your app.
app.source.title: Source
app.source.description: Provide information about the source of your application.
app.source.privateRegistryHint: Only required if your image is stored in a private registry.
app.health.title: Health Check Settings
app.health.description: Configure health checks so k3s can automatically monitor when your application is fully started and ready to receive traffic. In Kubernetes terms, these are startup, readiness and liveness probes.
app.health.httpPathHint: HTTP path on which a health check is performed. If the status code is in the 200-399 range, the container is considered healthy.
app.health.httpPortHint: Name or number of the port to access on the container.
app.health.schemeHint: Scheme to use for connecting to the container. Defaults to HTTP.
app.health.httpSchemeHttp: "HTTP" means that the scheme used will be http://
app.health.httpSchemeHttps: "HTTPS" means that the scheme used will be https://
app.health.headersHint: Custom headers to set in the request. HTTP allows repeated headers.
app.health.headerNameHint: The header field name. This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.
app.health.tcpPortHint: Port number to probe using TCP. The probe will attempt to open a TCP connection to this port. If it succeeds, the container is considered healthy.
app.health.intervalHint: How often, in seconds, to perform the health check. For example every 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.
app.health.timeoutHint: Number of seconds to wait for a connection to complete before timing out. Minimum value is 1.
app.health.failureThresholdHint: Number of consecutive failures required to mark the container as unhealthy. Minimum value is 1. Example: if interval is 15 seconds and failure threshold is 4, the container will be marked unhealthy after 1 minute.
app.storage.deleteDescription: The volume will be removed and the data will be lost. The changes will take effect after you deploy the app. Are you sure you want to remove this volume?
app.storage.detachDescription: The volume will be detached from the app. The data will remain on the cluster and can be re-attached later. The changes will take effect after you deploy the app. Are you sure you want to detach this volume?
app.storage.downloadDescription: The volume data will be zipped and downloaded. Depending on the size of the volume this can take a while. Are you sure you want to download the volume data?
app.storage.openFileBrowserDescription: To view the files of the volume, your app has to be stopped. The file browser will be opened in a new tab. Are you sure you want to open the file browser?
app.storage.stopAndOpenFileBrowser: Stop App and Open File Browser
app.storage.fileBrowserReadyDescription: The File Browser is ready and can be opened in a new tab.
app.storage.fileBrowserCredentials: Use the following credentials to log in:
app.storage.description: Add one or more volumes to configure persistent storage within your container.
app.storage.shareableTooltip: This volume can be mounted by other apps in this project
app.storage.sharedTooltip: This volume is mounted from another app's volume
app.storage.sharedCannotEdit: Shared volumes cannot be edited because size and storage class are inherited.
monitoring.nodes.fetchError: An error occurred while fetching current resource usage
monitoring.nodes.nodeResourcesDescriptio: Overview of all nodes in the cluster
app.container.title: Container Configuration
app.container.description: Override image defaults only when your workload needs custom startup behavior or Linux security settings.
app.container.runtimeDescription: Leave these fields empty to keep the command and arguments from the container image.
app.container.commandHint: Overrides the image ENTRYPOINT. Leave empty to keep the command defined by the container image.
app.container.argumentsHint: Overrides the image CMD. Add one item per argument in the order the process should receive them.
app.container.securityDescription: Change these values only when your image, mounted volumes, or tooling require specific Linux permissions.
app.container.runAsUserHint: Linux user ID for the main container process. Maps to runAsUser in the Kubernetes securityContext.
app.container.runAsGroupHint: Linux group ID for the main container process. Maps to runAsGroup in the Kubernetes securityContext.
app.container.fsGroupHint: Supplemental group ID applied at pod level so mounted volumes can be owned and writable by that group. Maps to fsGroup in the Kubernetes securityContext.
app.container.privilegedHint1: Removes most container isolation. The container gets all Linux capabilities, access to host devices, and can interact with the node almost like a root process on the host.
app.container.privilegedHint2: If the container is compromised, it can affect the Kubernetes node and other workloads. Use this only for workloads such as Docker-in-Docker or low-level system tooling.
app.container.privilegedWarning: Enable this only if you fully understand the implications and risks.
app.storage.editDescription: Configure your custom volume for this container.
app.storage.sharedResizeWarning: When changing the size of a shared volume, ensure that all apps using this volume are shut down before deploying the changes.
app.storage.accessModeHint1: In most cases you will want to use ReadWriteOnce. This means that the volume can be mounted only by a single container instance.
app.storage.accessModeHint2: If you want to run multiple instances or replicas of the same container, you will need to use ReadWriteMany. This allows multiple container instances to use the same storage on the same volume.
app.storage.accessModeHint3: After creation the access mode cannot be changed.
app.storage.cannotChangeAfterCreation: This cannot be changed after creation.
app.storage.storageClassHint1: Choose where the volume is provisioned.
app.storage.localPathHint: stores data on the master node and works only in single-node clusters.
app.storage.allowOtherAppsAttach: Allow other apps to attach this volume
settings.groups.canAccessBackupsDescript: If enabled, users can access the backups page and download backups from all apps.
